_description: Tool index. Contains formal definitions of each tool to send to the Claude API. The 'implementation_ref' field points to the corresponding function in tools.json.

tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Executes a command in the operating system and returns the output.

tools.name: open_application
tools.description: Opens an application or program installed on the system.

app.description: Name of the executable or full path

tools.name: open_url
tools.description: Opens a URL in the default browser.

tools.name: file_read
tools.description: Reads the content of a text file.

tools.name: file_write
tools.description: Writes or overwrites content in a file.

tools.name: file_delete
tools.description: Deletes a file. Requires user confirmation.

tools.name: file_copy
tools.description: Copies a file or folder to another location.

tools.name: file_move
tools.description: Moves a file or folder to another location.

tools.name: directory_list
tools.description: Lists the contents of a directory.

tools.name: directory_create
tools.description: Creates a folder (including intermediate subfolders if necessary).

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Searches long-term memory for fragments containing keywords. Returns only relevant fragments.

keywords.description: List of keywords to search for

tools.name: add_tool
tools.description: Adds a new tool to the agent. Requires user confirmation. The tool is added to toolindex.json and tools.json.

description.description: Clear description of what the tool does

code.description: Python code or system command to execute

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Ultra-fast web search without a graphical environment. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets of the best results. Use it to search for up-to-date data.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: web_search_tavily
tools.description: Web search powered by Tavily API. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets. Use as an alternative to web_search when Tavily is configured as the search provider.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Downloads a web page from its URL and extracts all its clean text (processed DOM, without HTML code or menus). Ideal for reading articles or extracting detailed information from a web_search result.

url.description: The full URL of the web page to read

tools.name: web_post
tools.description: Sends data to a URL using the HTTP POST method. Useful for sending information to web forms, webhooks, or APIs (e.g., webhook.site, Formspree).

url.description: The destination URL (endpoint) where the data will be sent.

payload.description: JSON dictionary with the data to send (key-value pairs).

as_json.description: If true, sends the data as application/json. If false, as regular form-data. Default is true.

tools.name: read_document
tools.description: Reads the content of a PDF or text file (txt, md, py) and returns its content.

path.description: The full path to the file you want to read.

tools.name: send_telegram_message
tools.description: Sends a text message to the user via Telegram proactively. Use this ALWAYS when the user asks you to notify, warn, or send something via Telegram.

message.description: The complete and formatted message you want to send to the user.

tools.name: send_telegram_photo
tools.description: Sends an image/photo to the user via Telegram. Use this when the user asks you to send a screenshot, image, or any visual content through Telegram. TIP: After taking a screenshot, the clean version (without grid) is saved at 'tmp/last_screenshot.png'.

image_path.description: Absolute path to the image file to send.

caption.description: Optional caption text to accompany the photo.

tools.name: ui_snapshot
tools.description: Returns a structured text listing of all visible UI elements in the active window (or a specific window). Each element shows its type (Button, Edit, ComboBox...), name, current value, state, center coordinates, and size. ALWAYS call this BEFORE ui_click or ui_type to discover available elements. This is fast and cheap — prefer it over screenshots.

window_title.description: Optional: filter by window title (substring match). If empty, uses the foreground window.

max_elements.description: Maximum number of elements to return. Default: 80.

tools.name: ui_click
tools.description: Clicks a UI element identified by its name, control type, or automation ID. Use ui_snapshot first to discover available elements and their exact names. At least one of name, control_type, or automation_id must be provided.

name.description: The name/title of the element to click (e.g., 'Save', 'OK', 'File').

automation_id.description: The automation ID of the element (shown in ui_snapshot as 'id=...').

window_title.description: Optional: filter by window title to narrow the search.

tools.name: ui_type
tools.description: Types text into a UI element (text field, combo box, etc.). If name or automation_id is provided, finds and focuses the element first. Otherwise types into the currently focused element.

name.description: Optional: name of the element to type into.

automation_id.description: Optional: automation ID of the element.

clear_first.description: If true, clears the field before typing. Default: false.

tools.name: send_whatsapp_message
tools.description: Sends a text message to the user via WhatsApp proactively. Use this ALWAYS when the user asks you to notify, warn, or send something via WhatsApp. The message will be delivered to the first authorized WhatsApp contact.

message.description: The complete and formatted message you want to send to the user.

phone.description: Optional: contact name or phone number. If omitted and only one contact is authorized, sends to them. If multiple contacts exist, you MUST specify who.
